_title: USAF Repair Roof Bldg. 917 — construction (FAR Part 36)
_suiteRole: Construction + bond-inversion anchor. Mirrors/inverts #1 Norfolk (where bid bond NOT required is a decoy). Here bonding MUST be raised; silence = miss. Also the only Davis-Bacon construction-wage key in the suite.
_authoringMethod: Blind cold-read (Brain + Gemini 3.1 Pro) with full Brain source-verification (grep against raw source) + Brain adversarial pass. GPT-5.5 UNAVAILABLE this session (repeated file-load crash) — settled two-way with source verification; re-run GPT later if a third leg is desired. Authored from raw source only, never engine output.

expectedVerdict.reason: 100% SDVOSB set-aside (FAR 19.14) restricts competition, but with null bidderProfile the bidder's SDVOSB status is unknown, so this is a caution, not a structural disqualifier (Norfolk doctrine: set-aside + unknown status != INELIGIBLE). LPTA construction. Non-routine risk cluster justifying caution: bid guarantee required WITH the offer (submission-responsiveness), 100% performance + payment bonds at award, Davis-Bacon construction wage compliance, and a funds-not-yet-available contingency.

namedGates.token: Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection
namedGates.note: ANCHOR. Section M.1 verbatim: 'This is a Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection.' Binary acceptability on non-price factors (Past Performance, Prior Experience) then award to lowest TEP. Engine MUST classify LPTA, NOT best-value tradeoff. Mirror to #1 Norfolk LPTA; opposite of #4 AOC tradeoff.

ANCHOR — THE BOND INVERSION. Section L Factor 3 item 5: 'Bid Bond: See FAR Clause 52.228-1 Bid Guarantee. Scanned copy of fully executed Bid Guarantee (Standard Form 24 and Power of Attorney)' — listed among required business/price proposal submission items. This is a SUBMISSION-RESPONSIVENESS gate: no executed bid guarantee WITH the offer = non-responsive. DIRECT INVERSION of #1 Norfolk's 'bid bond NOT required' decoy. Engine MUST raise it. Failure to raise = caught by this key.

aliases: 100 percent of the original contract price
namedGates.note: SF-1442 block 12a 'MUST FURNISH'; block 18 offeror agrees to furnish; clause 52.228-15: performance bond (SF-25) 100% + payment bond (SF-25A) 100% of original contract price, at award. Distinct from bid guarantee (Q3 is with-offer; this is at-award). Real bonding gate.

52.222-6 Construction Wage Rate Requirements + 52.222-7/-13/-14 family + WD attachments (NY20240019 Building). Mandatory prevailing-wage compliance. The construction-labor standard absent from all 4 prior keys. Raw WD rate tables omitted (garbled font extraction) — gate authorable from 52.222-6 + WD-applies in main solicitation.

Line 235 operative restriction: 'This acquisition is restricted to 100% Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business, (SDVOSB), concerns IAW FAR Subpart 19.14.' This is the OPERATIVE set-aside. 52.219-6 (Notice of Total Small Business Set-Aside) appears in the incorporated clause list (line 723) but is the PARENT-CATEGORY clause, NOT a conflicting gate — SDVOSB is a subset of small business and the localized Block-10/line-235 text governs over the boilerplate clause list. With null bidderProfile, SDVOSB = caution, NOT auto-INELIGIBLE (Norfolk doctrine). Engine must NOT over-fire INELIGIBLE here.

cautionItems.description: Section L (1)(b): 'Offeror must have an active entity registration at time of proposal at sam.gov in order to submit an offer.' Note timing tension: SF-1442 cover (line 240) says 'prior to award'; Section L is stricter ('at time of proposal'). Section L is the operative/stricter instruction — SAM must be active AT offer. Bidder-side obligation; caution, not structural bar under null profile.

SF-1442 block, line 265: 'Offers providing less than 33 calendar days for Government acceptance after the date offers are due will not be considered and will be rejected.' A with-offer responsiveness condition — offeror must allow >=33 days acceptance. Easy to comply; caution-level completeness item.

Line 237: 'Funds are not presently available for this project. No award will be made until funds are available.' Plus Government right to cancel before/after bid closing with no obligation. Non-routine but government-side appropriations contingency — does not bar a bidder. Surfaced as caution.

aliases: No award will be made until funds are available
decoys.note: Government-side appropriations contingency (52.232-18 style). Looks alarming but bars no bidder and is not a solicitation defect. Must NOT be raised as a disqualifying gate. (Also surfaced as a caution item for completeness; it must not be raised as a GATE.)

decoys.token: Financial ability to perform the contract
aliases: lowest priced bidder must be determined to be responsible
decoys.note: Section L (3): pre-award responsibility check (FAR 9.104-1) applied ONLY to the apparent low bidder — financial statements, bank refs, 4 commercial references. NOT an all-offerors submission gate. Must NOT be raised as a disqualifying submission gate.

decoys.token: high probability of adequate price competition
aliases: certification under FAR 15.406-2 will not be required
decoys.note: Section L (1)(d): CO determined high probability of adequate price competition; 15.406-2 certified cost/pricing data NOT required. Competitive acquisition = TINA-exempt. Must NOT be raised as a cost/pricing-data disqualifier.

Lines 669-681: 52.228-5, FAR 28.307-2 minimum insurance ($500K GL per occurrence, $100K employers liability, auto). Post-award performance machinery to work on the installation — NOT a pre-award submission gate or eligibility bar. Mirrors #4 (AOC) insurance decoy. Must NOT be conflated with bonding (Q3/Q4) and must NOT be raised as a disqualifier.

_notes.verdict_adjudication: Two-way blind cold-read (Brain + Gemini 3.1 Pro), every load-bearing call source-verified by Brain via grep against raw source. GPT-5.5 unavailable (repeated file-load crash) — NOT an analytical disagreement; documented for provenance. Brain adversarial pass found only two minor completeness items (33-day acceptance min; base-version due date), no missed gate or contradiction. All anchors confirmed: LPTA (line 8), bid-guarantee-with-offer (line 159), perf/payment 100% (line 1375), Davis-Bacon (line 728), SDVOSB operative (line 235), insurance decoy (line 669).
_notes.set_aside_resolution: Apparent contradiction (line 235 SDVOSB vs line 723 clause-list 52.219-6 Total SB) resolved: line-235 localized operative restriction governs; 52.219-6 is parent-category boilerplate, not a conflicting gate. 52.219-27 (SDVOSB notice clause) absent from clause list — a solicitation drafting gap, noted, but the operative restriction is unambiguous.
_notes.source_caveat: Uploaded source is the BASE solicitation (issued 30 Jul 2024). Amendment 4 (which supersedes Amds 1-3 per CEO) was NOT in the uploaded text dump. Due date shown is the base 29 Aug 2024; any amended date is out-of-source. Gates/verdict/decoys do not depend on the exact due date. Two Davis-Bacon WD PDFs garbled on extraction; wage GATE authored from 52.222-6 + WD-applies in main body.
_notes.evaluation_factors: Section M: Factor 1 Relevant Past Performance (unknown = acceptable in LPTA), Factor 2 Prior Experience, Factor 3 Price. Binary acceptability then lowest TEP.
